2605.02600.title: CoRAL: Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based Control for Robotic Manipulation
2605.02600.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs) demonstrate remarkable capabilities in high-level reasoning and semantic understanding, applying them directly to contact-rich manipulation remains a challenge due to their lack of explicit physical grounding and inability to perform adaptive control. To bridge this gap, we propose CoRAL (Contact-Rich Adaptive LLM-based control), a modular framework that enables zero-shot planning by decoupling high-level reasoning from low-level control. Unlike black-box policies, CoRAL uses LLMs not as direct controllers, but as cost designers that synthesize context-aware objective functions for a sampling-based motion planner (MPPI). To address the ambiguity of physical parameters in visual data, we introduce a neuro-symbolic adaptation loop: a VLM provides semantic priors for environmental dynamics, such as mass and friction estimates, which are then explicitly refined in real time via online system identification, while the LLM iteratively modulates the cost-function structure to correct strategic errors based on interaction feedback. Furthermore, a retrieval-based memory unit allows the system to reuse successful strategies across recurrent tasks. This hierarchical architecture ensures real-time control stability by decoupling high-level semantic reasoning from reactive execution, effectively bridging the gap between slow LLM inference and dynamic contact requirements. We validate CoRAL on both simulation and real-world hardware across challenging and novel tasks, such as flipping objects against walls by leveraging extrinsic contacts. Experiments demonstrate that CoRAL outperforms state-of-the-art VLA and foundation-model-based planner baselines by boosting success rates over 50% on average in unseen contact-rich scenarios, effectively handling sim-to-real gaps through its adaptive physical understanding.
2605.02600.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）和視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在高階推理和語義理解方面展現出卓越的能力，但由於缺乏明確的物理基礎和無法執行自適應控制，將它們直接應用於接觸豐富的操作仍然是一個挑戰。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了CoRAL（基於接觸豐富的自適應LLM控制），這是一個模組化框架，通過將高階推理與低階控制解耦來實現零樣本規劃。與黑箱策略不同，CoRAL並不將LLMs作為直接控制器，而是作為成本設計者，為基於取樣的運動規劃器（MPPI）合成上下文感知的目標函數。為了解決視覺數據中物理參數的模糊性，我們引入了一個神經-符號適應迴路：VLM提供環境動力學的語義先驗，例如質量和摩擦估計，這些估計隨後通過在線系統識別在實時中明確地進行精煉，而LLM則根據互動反饋迭代調整成本函數結構以修正策略錯誤。此外，基於檢索的記憶單元允許系統在重複任務中重用成功的策略。這種分層架構通過將高階語義推理與反應執行解耦，確保了實時控制的穩定性，有效地填補了緩慢的LLM推理與動態接觸需求之間的差距。我們在模擬和現實硬體上驗證了CoRAL，針對挑戰性和新穎的任務，例如利用外部接觸將物體翻轉至牆壁。實驗表明，CoRAL在未見的接觸豐富場景中，平均提高了成功率超過50%，有效地通過其自適應的物理理解處理了模擬到現實的差距，超越了最先進的VLA和基於基礎模型的規劃基準。
2605.02600.author: Berk Çiçek et.al.
2605.02600.authors: Berk Çiçek, Mert K. Er, Özgür S. Öğüz
